title: chewy savory chinese beef wonton-shoyu soup
description: hearty, comforting soup originating from xinjiang province, china, featuring chewy beef wontons and rich soy sauce broth.

ingredients.name: bonito flakes
ingredients.notes: nut-free-friendly alternative to traditional fish sauce.

ingredients.name: wonton wrappers
ingredients.notes: available in most chinese grocery stores.

ingredients.name: star anise
ingredients.notes: optional, for added depth of flavor.

instructions: in a large pot, combine soy sauce, sesame oil, ginger, garlic, and 2 cups of water.
bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes.
in a separate pan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat and cook the feather blade beef until browned, about 5 minutes.
add the shiitake mushrooms to the pan and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 3 minutes.
add the cooked beef and mushrooms to the pot with the soy sauce mixture, along with the remaining 2 cups of water.
add the wonton wrappers to the pot, one at a time, and cook until they float to the surface, about 5 minutes.
add the bonito flakes and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
taste and adjust the seasoning with sugar if necessary.
serve the soup hot, garnished with scallions and a sprinkle of bonito flakes.
